claim: Whole brain radiotherapy reduces the occurrence of new brain metastases.

result.claim: Whole brain radiotherapy reduces the occurrence of new brain metastases.
result.reasoning: The claim is supported by evidence from paper ID 205268782, titled "Adjuvant whole-brain radiation therapy after surgical resection of single brain metastases." This retrospective study of 358 patients found that Whole Brain Radiation Therapy (WBRT) significantly reduced distant brain site recurrence (HR = 0.43, 95% CI 0.30-0.61, P < .001). The abstract further states that "Adjuvant WBRT significantly reduces local and distant recurrences in subsets of patients, particularly those with metastases >3 cm or with active systemic disease." This directly indicates that WBRT reduces the occurrence of new brain metastases. While the study is retrospective and more corroborating evidence from other high-quality studies would increase confidence, the findings are clear and directly relevant to the claim.

evidence.text: The use of WBRT plus SRS did not improve survival for patients with 1 to 4 brain metastases compared with SRS alone.
evidence.source: Stereotactic radiosurgery plus whole-brain radiation therapy vs stereotactic radiosurgery alone for treatment of brain metastases: a randomized controlled trial.

evidence.text: The study investigated potential prognostic factors in patients treated with whole-brain irradiation for brain metastases from malignant melanoma, renal cell carcinoma, and colorectal cancer.
evidence.source: Prognostic factors for outcomes after whole-brain irradiation of brain metastases from relatively radioresistant tumors: a retrospective analysis

evidence.text: Patients had between 1 to 4 brain metastases.
evidence.source: Stereotactic radiosurgery plus whole-brain radiation therapy vs stereotactic radiosurgery alone for treatment of brain metastases: a randomized controlled trial.

evidence.text: Only 1-3 brain metastases were associated with improved survival (p = 0.007).
evidence.source: Prognostic factors for outcomes after whole-brain irradiation of brain metastases from relatively radioresistant tumors: a retrospective analysis

evidence.text: Patients with only 1-3 brain metastases were associated with improved local control (p < 0.001).
evidence.source: Prognostic factors for outcomes after whole-brain irradiation of brain metastases from relatively radioresistant tumors: a retrospective analysis

evidence.text: No extracerebral metastases were associated with improved survival (p < 0.001).
evidence.source: Prognostic factors for outcomes after whole-brain irradiation of brain metastases from relatively radioresistant tumors: a retrospective analysis

evidence.text: Secondary end points were brain tumor recurrence, salvage brain treatment, functional preservation, toxic effects of radiation, and cause of death.
evidence.source: Stereotactic radiosurgery plus whole-brain radiation therapy vs stereotactic radiosurgery alone for treatment of brain metastases: a randomized controlled trial.

evidence.text: Only 1-3 brain metastases were associated with improved local control (p < 0.001).
evidence.source: Prognostic factors for outcomes after whole-brain irradiation of brain metastases from relatively radioresistant tumors: a retrospective analysis

evidence.text: The study reviewed 358 patients with newly diagnosed, single brain metastases.
evidence.source: Adjuvant whole-brain radiation therapy after surgical resection of single brain metastases.

evidence.text: WBRT significantly reduced distant brain site recurrence (HR = 0.43, 95% CI 0.30-0.61, P < .001).
evidence.source: Adjuvant whole-brain radiation therapy after surgical resection of single brain metastases.
